title: The prefix increment/decrement form should be preferred
description: The prefix increment/decrement form should be usedAnalyze your codeintentionality-efficientmaintainabilityCode SmellperformanceWhy is this an issue?Postfix increment and decrement typically involves making a copy of the object being incremented or decremented, whereas its prefix form does not. Therefore the prefix form is usually the more efficient form, and should be preferred.This rule raises an issue if a postfix increment or decrement operator is used, but its return value is not read.Noncompliant code examplevoid myFunc(int lim) { int i; for (i = 0; i < lim; i++) { // do something } }Compliant solutionvoid myFunc(int lim) { int i; for (i = 0; i < lim; ++i) { // do something } }Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucke
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: The prefix increment/decrement form should be preferred
